title: Systems and methods for registering and tracking anatomical structures
abstract: An instrument registers and tracks a portion of a patient's anatomy by an Augmented Reality (AR) device worn by a surgeon during a surgical procedure. The instrument may be attached to the patient's anatomy. The instrument may be a tripod with a platform. A target plate having front and back faces may be securely attached to the platform. A two-dimensional (2D) code detectable by the AR device may be presented on the front and back faces of the target plate. The 2D code may define a coordinate system having a known relationship to a coordinate system for the patient's anatomy, e.g., through a transformation matrix. The AR device may utilize the coordinate system for the detected 2D code and the transformation matrix to present holograms in predetermined positions relative to the patient's anatomy.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS l. A registration and tracking device comprising: a frame including a hub and two arms extending from the hub; three legs attached to the frame to support the frame, wherein the legs are arranged to dock the registration and tracking device to a portion of an anatomical structure; and a target plate attached to the frame opposite the legs, wherein the target plate defines a front face and a back face, the target plate includes a two-dimensional code at the front face, the two-dimensional code is integrated with the target plate, the frame defines a nominal plane, and the target plate is attached to the frame substantially orthogonally to the nominal plane defined by the frame. 2. The registration and tracking device of claim 1 wherein the target plate is black anodized and the two-dimensional code is formed by laser etching the front face. 3. The registration and tracking device of claim 2 wherein the laser etching produces pixel elements by removing the black anodize and the pixel elements form the two-dimensional code. 4. The registration and tracking device of claim 2 wherein the target plate and the two-dimensional code are both approximately four inches by four inches. 5. The registration and tracking device of claim 4 wherein the two- dimensional code is a fourteen by fourteen array of the pixel elements. 6. The registration and tracking device of claim 5 wherein the two- dimensional code includes: a quiet zone around an outer perimeter of the two-dimensional code; a positioning detection marker; and a detection pattern. 7. The registration and tracking device of claim 6 wherein the two- dimensional code is free from including: one or more alignment markings; one or more timing patterns; version information; format information; and one or more error correction codes. 8. The registration and tracking device of claim 7 wherein the detection pattern does not encode data. 9. The registration and tracking device of claim 2 wherein the target plate is formed from aluminum. 10. The registration and tracking device of claim 1 wherein the target plate includes a second two-dimensional code at the back face, the second two-dimensional code is integrated with the target plate. 11. A registration and tracking device comprising: a support bar having first and second ends, a longitudinal axis, and a nominal plane; a platform slidably attached to the support bar; a first leg removably attached proximate to the first end of the support bar; a second leg removably and adjustably attached proximate to the second end of the support bar, where the second leg is adjustable in a direction along the longitudinal axis; 

 a third leg removably attached to the platform, where the third leg is adjustable in a direction orthogonal to the longitudinal axis; and a target plate attached to the platform opposite the first, second and third legs, wherein the target plate defines a front face and a back face, the target plate includes a two-dimensional code at the front face, the two-dimensional code is integrated with the target plate, the target plate is attached to the platform substantially orthogonally to the nominal plane. 12. The registration and tracking device of claim 11 wherein the target plate is black anodized and the two-dimensional code is formed by laser etching the front face. 13. The registration and tracking device of claim 12 wherein the laser etching produces pixel elements by removing the black anodize and the pixel elements form the two-dimensional code. 14. The registration and tracking device of claim 12 wherein the target plate and the two-dimensional code are both approximately four inches by four inches. 15. The registration and tracking device of claim 14 wherein the two- dimensional code is a fourteen by fourteen array of the pixel elements. 16. The registration and tracking device of claim 15 wherein the two- dimensional code includes: a quiet zone around an outer perimeter of the two-dimensional code; a positioning detection marker; and a detection pattern. 17. The registration and tracking device of claim 16 wherein the two- dimensional code is free from including: one or more alignment markings; one or more timing patterns; version information; format information; and one or more error correction codes. 18. The registration and tracking device of claim 17 wherein the detection pattern does not encode data. 19. The registration and tracking device of claim 12 wherein the target plate is formed from aluminum. 20. The registration and tracking device of claim 11 wherein the target plate includes a second two-dimensional code at the back face, the second two-dimensional code is integrated with the target plate. 21. A registration and tracking device comprising: a frame including a hub and two arms extending from the hub; three legs attached to the frame to support the frame, wherein the legs are arranged to dock the registration and tracking device to a portion of an anatomical structure; and a target plate attached to the frame opposite the legs, wherein the target plate defines a front face and a back face, the target plate includes a two-dimensional code at the front face, the two-dimensional code is integrated with the target plate, the three legs have tips, and the target plate is attached to the frame in a predetermined geometric relationship relative to the tips of the three legs.
